Title: Sign up Utente
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Sign Up page
2. Enter valid user details
3. Click on the Sign Up button
Expected Result: User account is created successfully

Title: Login Utente
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Login page
2. Enter valid user credentials
3. Click on the Login button
Expected Result: User is logged in successfully

Title: Aggiungere Segnalazione
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Report Addition page
2. Enter valid report details
3. Click on the Add Report button
Expected Result: Report is added successfully

Title: Visualizzazione Segnalazione
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Report Viewing page
2. Select a report
3. Verify the report details are displayed correctly
Expected Result: Report details are displayed correctly

Title: Modificare Segnalazione
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Report Viewing page
2. Select a report
3. Click on the Edit button
4. Update the report details
5. Click on the Save button
Expected Result: Report details are updated successfully

Title: Impostazioni Account
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Account Settings page
2. Verify the account details are displayed correctly
Expected Result: Account details are displayed correctly

Title: Sezione Educativa
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Educational Section page
2. Verify the educational resources are displayed correctly
Expected Result:
Educational resources are displayed correctly
These test cases are based on the explicit requirements written in the provided PDF. The PDF pages used for each test case are also mentioned.

Title: Sign up User
Preconditions: The user does not have an account registered.
Test Steps:
1. The user accesses the registration page and enters the required data.
2. The user submits the form.
Expected Result: The user receives a confirmation email.

Title: Login User
Preconditions: The user has a valid account.
Test Steps:
1. The user enters the login page and enters the required data.
2. The user submits the form.
Expected Result: The user is authenticated and redirected to the onboarding page.

Title: Add Report
Test Steps:
1. The user selects the option to create a new report.
2. The user enters the required details.
Expected Result: The report is saved and sent to the system.

Title: Type of Report
Preconditions: The user is in the process of creating a new report.
Test Steps: 1. The user selects the report type.
Expected Result: The report type is associated with the report.

Title: View Reports
Test Steps: 1. The user accesses the "View Reports" section from the main interface.
Expected Result: The user can view the recent reports on a map or in a list, with the option to filter by type or geographical area.

Title: Edit Report
Preconditions: The user has created a report and is authenticated.
Test Steps:
1. The user selects the option to edit one of their reports.
2. The user modifies the report details.
Expected Result: The report is updated in the system.

Title: Account Settings
Test Steps: 1. The user accesses the account settings.
Expected Result: The user can modify personal data, password, and profile preferences.

Title: Testing Collaboration Channels
Preconditions: Team members have access to email, Discord, WhatsApp, and Moodle.
Test Steps:
1. Send a message through email to the team.
2. Send a message through Discord to the team.
3. Send a message through WhatsApp to the team.
4. Post a message on Moodle for the team.
Expected Result: Messages are received and responded to by the team members within a reasonable time.

Title: Testing Roles and Responsibilities
Preconditions: Team members have defined roles and responsibilities.
Test Steps:
1. Verify that a Project Manager is assigned.
2. Verify that Team Leaders are assigned for each project area.
Expected Result: A Project Manager and Team Leaders are assigned and their roles are clearly defined.

Title: Testing Work Methodology
Preconditions: Team members are following the DevOps methodology.
Test Steps:
1. Verify that periodic testing is being performed.
2. Verify that errors are being minimized.
3. Verify that efficiency is being increased.
Expected Result: The team is following the DevOps methodology and the software development process is efficient.

Title: Testing Frequency and Types of Interaction
Preconditions: Team members are collaborating with each other and with external stakeholders.
Test Steps:
1. Verify that regular meetings are being held with the internal team.
2. Verify that external stakeholders (prof. A. Cortesi and A. Spanò) are consulted when necessary.
3. Verify that clients are being collaborated with effectively.
4. Verify that data is being managed securely and in compliance with privacy laws.
Expected Result: The team is collaborating effectively with all stakeholders and data is being managed securely.

Title: Testing Stakeholder Involvement
Preconditions: Stakeholders have been identified and their requirements have been gathered.
Test Steps:
1. Verify that user requirements have been gathered from user finales (citizens, tourists, and volunteers).
2. Verify that requirements from governmental and environmental organizations have been gathered.
3. Verify that the gathered requirements are being used to develop the app.
Expected Result: User and stakeholder requirements are being used to develop the app.

Title: Testing Requirement Classification
Preconditions: Requirements have been classified as Durevoli, Volatili, Funzionali, or NON Funzionali.
Test Steps:
1. Verify that Durevoli requirements are unchanging during the project.
2. Verify that Volatili requirements are subject to change during the project.
3. Verify that Funzionali requirements define specific functions of the system.
4. Verify that NON Funzionali requirements define qualities of the system.
Expected Result: Requirements are correctly classified as Durevoli, Volatili, Funzionali, or NON Funzionali.

Title: Testing Functional Requirements
Test Steps:
1. Verify that the system allows users to register with their name, surname, email, and password.
2. Verify that the system allows registered users to log in with their email and password.
3. Verify that the system allows users to log out of their active session.
4. Verify that users can add a new water quality report.
Expected Result: The system allows users to register, log in, log out, and add water quality reports.

Title: Testing Functional Requirements (Continued)
Test Steps:
1. Verify that the system allows users to view their own water quality reports.
2. Verify that the system allows users to edit their own water quality reports.
3. Verify that the system allows users to delete their own water quality reports.
Expected Result: The system allows users to view, edit, and delete their own water quality reports.

Title: Visualize Open Reports
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the application.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Reports section of the application.
2. Verify that the user can see a list of open reports.
3. Click on a report to view its details.
Expected Result: The user can see the report details including type, location, and date of the report.

Title: Modify User Profile
Test Data: New name, email, password, and preferences.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Profile section of the application.
2. Update the user's name, email, password, and preferences.
3. Save the changes.
Expected Result: The user's profile is updated with the new information.

Title: High Uptime and Offline Functionality
Test Steps: 1. Test the application's uptime and offline functionality.
Expected Result: The application has a high uptime and can manage reports even offline, syncing data once reconnected.

Title: Fast Loading Times
Test Steps: 1. Test the loading times of interactive maps and reports.
Expected Result: The loading times are fast, with interactive maps and reports loading within 3 seconds.

Title: Compatibility with Android Devices
Test Steps: 1. Test the application's compatibility with Android devices from version 8.1 (API 27) and up.
Expected Result: The application is compatible with at least 95% of Android devices from version 8.1 and up.

Title: User-Friendly Interface
Test Steps: 1. Test the user-friendliness of the application's interface.
Expected Result: The interface is intuitive and accessible, with no more than 5 steps required to complete a report.

Title: Automated Testing
Test Steps: 1. Test the application's automated testing process.
Expected Result: The automated testing process ensures quality and reduces errors and bugs in the code, with at least 95% test coverage.

Title: User Registration
Preconditions: User has access to the registration interface.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the registration interface.
2. Enter the Name, Surname, Email, and Password.
3. Click on the "Register" button.
Expected Result: The user's data is saved in the database, and the user is registered.

Title: User Login
Preconditions: User is registered and has valid credentials.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login interface.
2. Enter the Email and Password.
3. Click on the "Login" button.
Expected Result: The user is authenticated and has access to the protected features of the app.

Title: Reporting a Segnalazione
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the reporting interface.
2. Enter the Segnalazione details.
3. Click on the "Submit" button.
Expected Result: The Segnalazione is saved in the database and is visible to other users.

Title: View Open Emergencies in User's Area of Interest
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application as a user.
2. Navigate to the emergencies section.
3. Select the user's area of interest.
4. Verify that the open emergencies related to the selected area are displayed.
Expected Result: The user can view the open emergencies in their area of interest, including details such as type, location, and date.

Title: Update User Profile
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application as a user.
2. Navigate to the profile section.
3. Update the user's information.
4. Verify that the updated information is saved and displayed.
Expected Result: The user's profile information is updated, and the updated information is displayed.
